<!--
Thanks for contributing to ormguard! Keep PRs focused — one logical change per PR
is easier to review and revert. Fill in the sections below; delete any that don't apply.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change, and why? -->

## Related issue

<!-- e.g. "Closes #123". If there's no issue, briefly say why the change is needed. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior)
- [ ] Refactor / internal (no behavior change)
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] CI / tooling

## How was this tested?

<!-- Commands you ran, new tests added, and which databases/dialects you covered
     (SQLite / PostgreSQL / MySQL). ormguard behavior is dialect-sensitive. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass locally (`pytest`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`ruff check .`)
- [ ] Added / updated tests for the change
- [ ] Updated `CHANGELOG.md` under "Unreleased"
- [ ] Updated docs / README if behavior or the public API changed
- [ ] New checks are **opt-in** and default to a sensible severity (keeps false positives low)
